text.title: Steerco Meeting Playbook Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the steerco-meeting-playbook methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Pre-read pack must land in attendee inboxes minimum 3 business days (72h) before the meeting. Same-day pre-reads are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Senior stakeholders read pre-reads in transit, not live; <72h leads to 'let me get back to you' defers.

rule.statement: Every Steerco must include a numbered 'Asks' section. Each ask in the form 'We ask Steerco to approve/reject/defer X by date Y'. Vague asks ('guidance on Z') are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without explicit binary asks, the meeting ends with vague nods that no one is bound by.

rule.statement: If the asks list at T-5 days contains zero substantive decisions, the meeting must be cancelled and a written status memo sent instead.
rule.rationale: Reputational cost of cancelled-with-good-reason is far lower than the credibility cost of a 90-min status read-out.

rule.statement: A decision memo must be sent to all attendees within 48 hours of the meeting: each ask + decision + rationale + owner + deadline + deferrals.
rule.rationale: Steerco decisions are referenced months later; without a written memo, parties remember the meeting differently.

rule.statement: Agenda time must allocate ≤30% to status snapshot and ≥50% to asks + decisions.
rule.rationale: Steerco attendee time is the most expensive minute in the project's hour-economy; status is the pre-read's job.

list.item: Forum is decorative — sponsor delegates, no quorum, decisions made elsewhere
Project is in active rescue → daily war room + ad-hoc exec calls instead
No decisions outstanding for this cycle — cancel the meeting (rule r3)
A weekly status report already covers the audience — Steerco would duplicate
